Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion for leave to file motion to set aside default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) Fireman's Fund's motion to set aside default judgment is granted. The default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on September 10, 2015 is set aside pursuant to C.C.P. 473.5 and pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default judgment on the ground of mistake. Off Calendar Jan-06-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Patrick R. Campbell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Robert Lundquist
Hillary H. Huth of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / opposing party J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.
(Refer to Sign-in sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for order advancing current trial date with preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The trial date is advanced from October 19, 2015 to April 20, 2015 at 11:15am in Department 503. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. Last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 15, 2015. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice dictated by the C.C.P., unless plaintiff stipulates otherwise. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Before the Court will hear any discovery motion the parties involved in the discovery dispute must schedule and attend an informal discovery conference with the Court. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than February 13, 2015. Responses to the demand are due no later than February 20, 2015. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by April 14, 2015 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00a.m. on April 16, 2015. Fact discovery cut-off date is April 3, 2015. Expert discovery cut-off date is April 17, 2015. No later than January 9, 2015 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than January 9, 2015 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on March 11, 2015 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on April 20, 2015 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by April 16, 2015 at 2:00p.m. The 120 days, which is counted from the initial hearing date of December 23, 2014, is April 22, 2015. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
